ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-08-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard; PES-18302437 The estate of Nellie Green Johnson and PES-16-299943 The estate of Lena Mae Renfro.

Appearances: Patricia Rowe appearing via zoom for and with Valicia Helmer (petitioner) appearing via zoom.

The Court approves the request to reinstate Valicia Helmer as administrator of Lena's estate. The first and final accounting etc., and Second Amended petition for approval of report etc., are taken under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Ross Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Maloney. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-07-2022 Continued To Jun-07-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patricia Rowe, via Zoom, for Valicia Helmer, Personal Representative.

The court acknowledges counsel's recent filing and advises that it is not ready to make distributions at this time. Counsel reports on the pending probate in Mississippi and which has yet to be filed and advises that a continuance of four (4) months seems appropriate. Counsel offers advocacy for the court to consider allowing distributions. The court reiterates that it is not inclined to make preliminary distributions at this time.

The matter is continued to Jun-07-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-12-2022 Continued To Dec-07-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patricia Rowe, via Zoom, for Valicia Helmer, Administrator.

Counsel reports on the delays with this matter and that she is working with the Probate Examiner to resolve the pending issues with this matter. Counsel reports of taxes having been filed but that a Personal Representative needs to be appointed for the estate in Mississippi (MS) and is working with MS attorney Bobby Moakes on that issue. Counsel confirms that the proceeds from the sale of the Estate real property are now in insured accounts. Counsel asks the court to consider making a partial distribution from this estate instead of first making a distribution to the related Estate of Lena Mae Renfro. The court advises that it is not prepared to order partial distributions via an oral request and advises counsel to file papers accordingly.

The matter is continued to Dec-07-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Petition For Order Increasing Bond; Reappraisal Of Real Property
Off Calendar Jul-21-2021 Granted Per Written Order

PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE STATUS FOR ATTORNEY BOBBY MOAK
Off Calendar Jul-14-2021 Continued To Aug-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Sep-25-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Patricia Rowe appeared by zoom for Valicia Helmer (Petitioner).
Bobby Moak appeared by zoom for the Mississippi Beneficiaries.

Mr. Moak stated that he has no objection to the petition. Ms. Rowe stated that there have been no objections filed or stated. The Court asked what the basis is for the $460,000.00 value. Ms. Rowe stated that the property was appraised by a Probate Referee at the date of death of 1970. The Court stated that it is concerned about the amount of the bond being tied to a 50-year old value and noted that the inventory and appraisal is not on file. Ms. Rowe stated that she thought the inventory and appraisal was filed. Mr. Moak stated that he will get together with Ms. Rowe as to the value of the property. The Court informed Ms. Rowe that it could appoint her Client with bond at $460,000.00 with limited authority, or with a $1,000,000.00 bond and full authority. Ms. Rowe stated that it is better to have a $1,000,000.00 bond, that the property is the only asset of the estate and that it is to be sold. Mr. Moak stated the he has no objection. The Court informed Ms. Rowe that if she finds that the value of the property is higher, she needs to come in ex parte to have the bond increased and stated that it wants the present value of the property for the bond. Ms. Rowe clarified that they have an inventory and appraisal of the property in the related estate of Lena Mae Renfro (PES-16-299943). The Court will grant the petition with full authority. The lost will dated January 17, 1967 is admitted to probate. Bond is set at $1,000,000.00. Status on close of estate is set for January 24, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Ms. Rowe that she needs to have either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration filed one week prior to that date. The Amended Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters of Administration is under submission with the Court's intent to grant the petition.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jan-15-2020 Continued To Mar-11-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patricia Rowe appeared by telephone for Valicia Helmer.
Bobby Moak appeared by telephone for Mississippi Heirs.

The Court noted that it doesn't have a response to the Probate Examiner's notes and stated that it does not know how any Court can decide who the Heirs are without determining if the will governs, or it is intestate. The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is continued to March 11, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to file briefs in response to all of the issues raised in the Probate Examiner's notes.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Lelu. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Aug-27-2019 Continued To Oct-01-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patricia Rowe appeared by telephone for Valicia Helmer (Petitioner).
Bobby Moak appeared by telephone for the Mississippi Heirs.

The Court stated that the pro hac vice motion appears to be in order, but it does not have a proposed order. Mr. Moak stated that he filed the order through file and serve, and that he is having a runner bring one in today. The Court reminds all parties that were present in the courtroom that proposed orders do not get filed and that submitting papers through e-file does not get hard copies to the Court. Ms. Rowe stated that she filed an amended petition a couple days ago and that after looking at the Examiner's notes she will have to amend it again. The Court noted that nothing is showing on the register of actions. Ms. Rowe stated that she will look into it and make sure it gets filed, and that they still have to look for the representatives for the estates of the deceased people and for dates of death. The Court granted the Motion to Admit Counsel Pro Hac Vice (subject to receiving and reviewing the order). The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is continued to October 1, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court is expecting that Mr. Moak is able to fully participate and to have amended petition filed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Aug-28-2019 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jun-25-2019 Continued To Aug-27-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patricia Rowe appeared by telephone for Valicia Helmer (Petitioner).
Bobby Moak appeared by telephone for Mississippi Heirs.

The Court noted that it has not seen Mr. Moak's application to appear pro hac vice. Mr. Moak stated that he was under the impression that Attorney Amanda Ebey had filed it. Ms. Rowe stated that the original will has not been found, that she will have to amend the petition to reflect a lost will, that the Probate Examiner's notes indicated that they had not provided dates of death for any of the Decedent's Children or other deceased people, and that they don't know a lot of the deceased people. Ms. Rowe also asked if they need death certificates for all of them, stated that there is no Personal Representative for the estate of Ernestine and that it never went through probate. The Court stated that it can not provide legal advice, that there needs to be a legal basis for distribution to a party and that legal authority needs to be properly established. Ms. Rowe also raised the question of publication and stated that she needs at least 60-days. The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is continued to August 27, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar May-21-2019 Continued To Jun-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patricia Rowe appeared by telephone for Valicia Helmer (Petitioner).
Bobby Moak appeared by telephone for the Mississippi Heirs.
Amanda Ebey sponsoring Mr. Moak on his pro hac vice application.

Mr. Moak stated that he filed for pro hac vice yesterday. Ms. Ebey stated that she has been in contact with Mr. Moak and that she will be sponsoring him to appear pro hac vice in this matter. The Court informed Mr. Moak that his document may be rejected because it was not properly presented. The Court noted that there are inconsistent allegations with regard to will and that the petition will probably need to be amended. Ms. Rowe stated that originally when they first started the petition they only had a copy of the will, that she filed a supplemental declaration, and that Mr. Moak and his Clients found the original will. Ms. Rowe also stated that the original will was mailed to the Court and that the Clerk's office said that do not have it, but there is record of the Court having received it. The Court stated that either way the petition will need to be amended, but it is not sure in what way and that it will continue the matter for Mr. Moak to file his application to appear pro hac vice. Ms. Rowe questions whether her original petition and declaration stand if the will is not found. The Court stated that the facts originally stated are no longer true and that it is not sure if it will require amendment or a supplemental declaration. The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is continued to June 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar May-14-2019 Continued To May-21-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Apr-30-2019 Continued To May-14-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bobby Moak appeared by telephone for the Missippi Heirs.

Mr. Moak stated that twenty four persons have a benefit in this estate and that he is in the process of filing a request to appear Pro Hac Vice. The Court noted that the Petitioner and the Attorney for the Petitioner are not present. Mr. Moak stated that his Clients are not planning on objecting as to the letters of administration. The Court stated that there are problems with the petition, that it is not ready to go forward, that there is no response to the Probate Examiner's notes, that the petition has not been amended to request administration of a lost will and amongst other issues. The Court also informed Mr. Moak that if he had not called in, it would have probably taken the petition off calendar and asked Mr. Moak if he preferred a continuance. Mr. Moak stated that he is not objecting to a continuance. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to May 14, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the Petitioner to amend and supplement her petition. If nothing happens by then, this petition will go off calendar. Mr. Moak is free to communicate the Court's position with Counsel.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
